title: fix(enter): reflect CORS request headers so OpenAI SDKs work from browsers
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Opening the CORS gates
gist.blurb: We taught our API to play nice with OpenAI SDKs in the browser. No more pesky CORS errors blocking your web apps from buzzing along!
gist.summary: Updated CORS middleware to accept custom telemetry headers, enabling standard OpenAI SDKs to work directly from the browser.
gist.impact: Developers can now build client-side web apps using the OpenAI SDK without hitting preflight header restrictions.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a friendly gatekeeper, unlocking a digital gate or padlock for a browser/SDK robot. Represents removing the CORS barrier.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny security guard hat, happily unlocking a glowing lime green (#ecf874) padlock on a digital gate. A chunky pixel robot with a browser window for a face waits excitedly. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
